questions.question: In triangle $DEF$, point $G$ (not shown) lies on $\overline{DE}$. The sides $DF = 4$ and $FE = 3$. If the measure of $\angle DFG$ is $x^{\circ}$, and the measure of $\angle GFE$ is $y^{\circ}$, what is the value of $\cos x^{\circ} - \sin y^{\circ}$?
questions.explanation: Since $x + y$ equals $\angle DFE$, and $G$ lies on $DE$, angles $x$ and $y$ are complementary parts of the full angle at $F$. In this right triangle configuration, $\cos x = \sin y$, so $\cos x - \sin y = 0$.

questions.question: According to a US law, ramps must form an angle with level ground such that $\tan \theta < \dfrac{1}{12}$. If the ramp has a height of 22.3 inches, what is the least possible value of $x$ (the horizontal distance), in inches?
questions.explanation: At the limit, $\tan \theta = \dfrac{1}{12} = \dfrac{22.3}{x}$. So $x = 22.3 \times 12 = 267.6$. Since $\tan \theta$ must be strictly less than $\dfrac{1}{12}$, the least integer value is $x = 268$.

questions.question: According to a US law, ramps must form an angle with ground such that $\tan \theta \leq \dfrac{1}{12}$. If the ramp height is 28.2 inches, what is the least possible value of $x$ (the horizontal distance), in inches?
questions.explanation: $\tan \theta = \dfrac{28.2}{x} \leq \dfrac{1}{12}$. So $x \geq 28.2 \times 12 = 338.4$. Least possible value is $x = 339$ (since $x$ must satisfy the inequality and presumably is a whole number, though 338.4 works if decimals allowed). Per answer key: 339.

questions.question: In the triangle shown, $AC = 53$, $AB = 54$, and $\angle C = 90^{\circ}$. What is the value of $\cos A$?
In the triangle shown with sides 14 and 27, and angle $x$ between them (right angle at the bottom). What is the value of $\cos x$?
In $\triangle ABC$, $\angle B$ is a right angle and $BC = 204$ mm. If $\cos A = \dfrac{3}{5}$, what is the length, in millimeters, of $\overline{AB}$?
In right triangle $RST$, $R + S = 90^{\circ}$. The value of $\sin(R) = \dfrac{3\sqrt{17}}{13}$. What is the value of $\cos(S)$?
The measure of angle $C$ is $\dfrac{5\pi}{4}$ radians. What is the value of $\cos C$?
questions.explanation: $\dfrac{5\pi}{4} = 225^{\circ}$, which is in the third quadrant. $\cos 225^{\circ} = -\dfrac{\sqrt{2}}{2}$.

questions.question: Triangle $XYZ$ is a right triangle with $XZ = 29$, $ZY = 37$, and $\angle Z = 90^{\circ}$. Which of the following has the same value as $\sin X$?
questions.explanation: In a right triangle, $\sin X = \cos Y$ since $X + Y = 90^{\circ}$.

questions.question: In the triangle shown, $RT = 79$, $\angle S = 90^{\circ}$, and $RS = \sqrt{157}$. What is the value of $\sin R$?
$2\cos(90-a)^{\circ} \cdot \cos b^{\circ} + \sin(a+y)^{\circ} \cdot \sin(90-b)^{\circ}$. Given $\sin a^{\circ} = 0.5$, $\cos b^{\circ} = 0.99$, and $0 < b < 90$. What is the value when $y = 0$?
Right triangle $ABC$ has $AC = 12$, $AB = 24$, and $\angle C = 90^{\circ}$. What is the value of $\sin A$?
In the triangle shown, $RT = 53$, $\angle S = 90^{\circ}$, and $RS = \sqrt{105}$. What is the value of $\sin R$?
Triangle $XYZ$ is a right triangle with $XZ = 23$, $ZY = 28$, and $\angle Z = 90^{\circ}$. Which has the same value as $\sin X$?
In the right triangle shown with legs 22 and 39, and hypotenuse $c$. The value of $\sin x^{\circ} = \dfrac{k}{c}$. What is the value of $k$?
In the right triangle shown with legs 28 and 35, and hypotenuse $c$. The value of $\sin x^{\circ} = \dfrac{k}{c}$. What is the value of $k$?
In triangle $JKL$, $\cos(K) = \dfrac{36}{60}$ and angle $J$ is a right angle. What is the value of $\cos L$?
questions.explanation: Since $K + L = 90^{\circ}$ (as $J = 90^{\circ}$), $\cos L = \sin K$. $\cos K = \dfrac{36}{60} = \dfrac{3}{5}$, so $\sin K = \dfrac{4}{5}$. Thus $\cos L = \dfrac{4}{5}$.

questions.question: In the right triangle shown with legs 39 and 40, and angle $x^{\circ}$ at the top. The value of $\tan x^{\circ} = \dfrac{c}{39}$. What is the value of $c$?
In right triangle $RST$, $R + S = 90^{\circ}$. The value of $\sin(R) = \dfrac{2\sqrt{10}}{7}$. What is the value of $\cos(S)$?
The measure of angle $B$ is $\dfrac{3\pi}{4}$ radians. What is the value of $\sin B$?
questions.explanation: $\dfrac{3\pi}{4} = 135^{\circ}$, which is in the second quadrant. $\sin 135^{\circ} = \dfrac{\sqrt{2}}{2}$.

questions.question: According to a US law, ramps must form an angle with level ground such that $\tan \theta < \dfrac{1}{12}$. If the ramp has a height of 29.1 inches, what is the least possible value of $x$ (the horizontal distance), in inches?
In triangle $QRS$, $\angle R = 90^{\circ}$, $RS = 47$, and $QR < RS$. Which expression represents the length of $QS$?
For two acute angles, $\angle Q$ and $\angle R$, $\cos(Q) = \sin(R)$. The measures are $x + 61$ and $4x + 4$ degrees, respectively. What is the value of $x$?
In triangle $XYZ$, $\angle Y = 90^{\circ}$, $\angle Z = 39^{\circ}$, and $YZ = 22$ units. If the area can be represented by $k\tan 39^{\circ}$, what is the value of $k$?
